user_prompt:
Clean technical schematic of the Spatial Graph (SG) neural-network architecture (FlexibleGATNet) for fNIRS classification. Publication-quality monochrome boxes-and-arrows with ONE accent color (teal). White background. Sans-serif labels. All tensor shapes in monospace. Wide horizontal flow, no 3D rendering, no photorealism.

Boxes left-to-right with arrows between:

(1) INPUT GRAPH: small graph icon (8-12 nodes with edges) labeled 'G = (V, E)'. Annotation below: nodes = 23 channels, x_i in R^6 (mean, min, max, skewness, kurtosis, variance); edges = (i,j) iff |corr| >= 0.1; edge_attr in R^2 = (coherence, |corr|).
Tensor labels: 'X: [B*23, 6]' and 'edge_attr: [|E|, 2]'.

(2) GATv2 LAYER 1: rectangle labeled 'GATv2Conv-1'. Inside: '8 heads, 112 filters/head, edge_dim=2'. Below: '+ residual proj, ELU, dropout 0.3, batch-norm OFF'. Output shape annotation: '[B*23, 8*112=896]'.

(3) GATv2 LAYER 2: rectangle labeled 'GATv2Conv-2'. Inside: '6 heads, 80 filters/head, edge_dim=2'. Below: '+ residual proj, ELU, dropout 0.3'. Output: '[B*23, 6*80=480]'.

(4) GLOBAL MEAN POOL: small box 'global_mean_pool over 23 nodes'. Output: '[B, 480]'.

(5) FC: rectangle 'Linear(480, 224) + ELU + Dropout(0.3)'. Output: '[B, 224]'.

(6) CLASSIFIER: rectangle 'Linear(224, 2)'. Output in ACCENT color: '[B, 2] logits -> softmax -> {HC, GAD}'.

Below the whole figure, small caption: 'directed edges, self-loops, leak-free fold-wise standardisation of x and edge_attr'.

Avoid: brain anatomy, photographic textures, gradients, decorative icons. Avoid drawing actual matrix grids - just shape annotations as text. Use only sans-serif typography.

iterations.prompt:
Create a high-quality scientific diagram with these requirements:

VISUAL QUALITY:
- Clean white or light background (no textures or gradients)
- High contrast for readability and printing
- Professional, publication-ready appearance
- Sharp, clear lines and text
- Adequate spacing between elements to prevent crowding

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Clear, readable sans-serif fonts (Arial, Helvetica style)
- Minimum 10pt font size for all labels
- Consistent font sizes throughout
- All text horizontal or clearly readable
- No overlapping text

SCIENTIFIC STANDARDS:
- Accurate representation of concepts
- Clear labels for all components
- Include scale bars, legends, or axes where appropriate
- Use standard scientific notation and symbols
- Include units where applicable

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Colorblind-friendly color palette (use Okabe-Ito colors if using color)
- High contrast between elements
- Redundant encoding (shapes + colors, not just colors)
- Works well in grayscale

LAYOUT:
- Logical flow (left-to-right or top-to-bottom)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Balanced composition
- Appropriate use of whitespace
- No clutter or unnecessary decorative elements

IMPORTANT - NO FIGURE NUMBERS:
- Do NOT include "Figure 1:", "Fig. 1", or any figure numbering in the image
- Do NOT add captions or titles like "Figure: ..." at the top or bottom
- Figure numbers and captions are added separately in the document/LaTeX
- The diagram should contain only the visual content itself

Generate a publication-quality scientific diagram that meets all the guidelines above.

early_stop_reason: Quality score 7.5 meets threshold 8.5 for journal
